sessions.desc: Strength foundation - hip hinge, squat, single-leg work
Easy jogs on soft sand, 20–30 min. Feel the surface.
sessions.note: 1× per week. Effort should feel conversational.

sessions.desc: Swim or cycle for active recovery. Nothing sport-specific.
Ankle and hip work. Hamstring loading under fatigue.
Progressive overload - posterior chain priority. RDLs, box jumps, sled.
Easy aerobic runs 30–50 min. Also short hill repeats on sand.
sessions.note: 2–3× per week. Never going to threshold yet.

sessions.desc: Starts and acceleration drills, 30–40m. Not race pace.
sessions.note: 1× per week. Focus is mechanics not time.

sessions.desc: Aerobic interval work - beach flags circuits, 400–800m tempo runs.
6–8 × 60m at 90% effort. Full rest between.
sessions.note: 2× per week. This is where it starts to hurt.

sessions.desc: 3–4 × 90m at near-race pace. 4 min recovery.
sessions.note: 1× per week only. Don't cook legs mid-week.

sessions.desc: Maintain strength, reduce volume. Power focus - jumps, sprint-specific lifts.
90m with a start and turn sequence. Timed runs.
sessions.note: Build confidence in the shape of the race.

sessions.desc: 8–10 × 90m at race pace. Full recovery - 5 min between.
sessions.note: Working toward your target set of 12.

sessions.desc: Short flags starts, 15m acceleration work, false-start simulation.
Power maintenance only. Reduced volume. No DOMS going into race week.
Turning mechanics, sand reading, heat race simulation.
4–5 × 90m, race pace, full rest. Leave feeling fresh not smashed.
sessions.note: Once in comp week. Not daily.

sessions.desc: Short burst starts, flags practice. 15–20 min total.
Serious rest. Sleep and nutrition now do more work than training.
Visualise heats. Walk the course. Understand the draw.
Technique focus. Drill sets, catch work. 2–3km easy.
sessions.note: 2× per week. Feel the stroke, not the clock.

sessions.desc: Easy beach swims, 800–1200m. Comfortable pace.
sessions.note: 1× per week if water is accessible.

sessions.desc: Lat pull, shoulder stability, rotator cuff. Not heavy.
Anything non-swimming. Bike, run, whatever you enjoy.
Steady 1500–3000m in open water. Navigation practice.
sessions.note: Weekly. Build distance week on week.

sessions.desc: Aerobic pyramid sets - 200, 400, 800, 400, 200. Low rest.
Sighting drills, catch-up drill, open water stroke tempo.
sessions.note: Every swim has a tech component.

sessions.desc: Pull-up progressions, core, hip flexor loading.
4–6 × 400m at CSS pace. 30–45 sec rest.
Continuous 2–3km at hard-sustainable effort. Track pace.
8 × 200m at target 400m pace. This builds you toward your set.
Maintain pulling strength. No new movements.
8 × 400m open water. Pace per rep should be race-goal pace.
sessions.note: Once weekly only. Full week to recover.

sessions.desc: Short fast reps - 6 × 100m at faster than race pace.
Single continuous 2–3km to maintain aerobic ceiling.
800m easy after hard days. Flush the legs.
4–5 × 400m, race pace. Reduce volume, keep speed.
4 × 100m fast. Keep neuromuscular sharpness.
Short open-water session. Practice sighting the comp course if possible.
Two full rest days before comp day.
Lat, rotator cuff, core stability. Paddling posture work.
Flat water, casual. 45–60 min. No intervals.
Cycling for aerobic base without shoulder load.
Thoracic rotation, hip flexor, shoulder CARs daily.
60–90 min continuous. Z2 effort - can hold a conversation.
6–8 × 3 min moderate effort, 2 min easy. Volume-based.
Stroke efficiency, catch timing, boat trim practice.
sessions.note: Every session has a focus cue.

sessions.desc: Paddle-specific pull work, rotational power development.
4–5 × 1000m at hard-sustainable effort. 3–4 min rest.
sessions.note: Build toward your target 8. Once weekly.

sessions.desc: 3 × 1000m at target race pace. Full recovery.
2 × 20 min tempo. Hold uncomfortable but sustainable effort.
Power maintenance. Reduce volume. Keep rotational strength.
8 × 1000m at race pace. Full 4–5 min recovery.
sessions.note: Once weekly. This is race simulation.

sessions.desc: 6 × 300m at faster than race pace. Sharpen the top end.
Easy 45 min paddle. Keep the engine warm.
Practise race starts, first 200m surges, drafting position.
4 × 1000m at race pace. Stop feeling tired, stay sharp.
4 × 300m fast. Maintain neuromuscular firing.
Check equipment. Paddle a course lap easy.
Rest is performance. Two easy days before comp.
Shoulder stability, core, pulling strength. Fundamentals.
Flat water cruise, 30–40 min. Technique focus.
Use surf to work wave reading and balance.
Bike, swim, run. Keeps aerobic base without paddle load.
45–60 min continuous at comfortable effort.
6 × 2 min on 1 min off. Moderate effort.
sessions.note: Builds capacity for harder work ahead.

sessions.desc: Prone paddling mechanics, turning, wave catch efficiency.
Lat and rotational power. No max effort yet.
4–5 × 600m at hard effort. 3 min rest.
3 × 600m at target race effort. Judge pace.
Continuous 3km at moderate pace.
Power phase. Jump, explosive pull. Reduce volume.
8 × 600m at race pace. 3–4 min full recovery.
6 × 200m at sprint pace. Faster than race speed.
Race turn practice. Buoy rounding, water starts.
30 min easy. Keep the engine ticking over.
4 × 600m race pace. Not 8, just 4. Stay fresh.
4 × 200m fast. Maintain the sharpness.
Board, fins, leash. Easy course paddle.
Two full days before race. Eat, sleep, stretch.
One session each: ocean swim, beach run, gym. Variety is the goal.
Full body strength. Don't neglect the gym in offseason - this is when you build it.
Treat this phase seriously. Legs and shoulders need genuine rest.
Complete JLA/LA if not done. Get cert requirements out of the way.
Long aerobic ocean swims weekly. 2km+ continuous.
Long ski or board paddle weekly. 60+ min.
sessions.note: Alternate ski and board across the week.

sessions.desc: Easy soft sand aerobic runs. 30–45 min.
General strength - pull, push, hinge, squat. No event specialisation yet.
4–5 × 400m at CSS. Builds your worst leg.
sessions.note: Iron athletes often lose races in the water.

sessions.desc: 4–5 × 1000m ski or 4–5 × 600m board.
sessions.note: One craft per session. Alternate across weeks.

sessions.desc: 6 × 90m soft sand. Legs need speed-specific work too.
sessions.note: Beach sprint is easy to neglect in iron prep.

sessions.desc: Swim-to-paddle transition practice. The change is where time is won/lost.
Swim-board/ski-beach run sequence. Not full distance, half of each.
Full swim target set one week, full paddle next.
sessions.note: Can't peak everything every week - rotate which event you hammer.

sessions.desc: Work toward 8–10 × 90m. Legs need to be ready even after swim and paddle.
Every 3rd week: reduce volume by 40%. Iron athletes accumulate fatigue.
Sharpen your strongest scoring event in final week. Do a half-version of the full set.
sessions.note: Know which event matters most for your team's points.

sessions.desc: Short activation session in each discipline. Not performance - just pattern.
sessions.note: One quality rep in each modality.

sessions.desc: Run the iron race in your head. Equipment, changeover, timing.
sessions.note: Most iron medals are won in transition.

sessions.desc: Two full days. For iron athletes this is even more important.
sessions.note: You have three engines to arrive fresh with.

sessions.desc: Kick sets, pull buoy, catch drills. 2km per session.
Practise tube and belt rescue sequences. Slow and deliberate.
sessions.note: You should know these perfectly before you add speed.

sessions.desc: Shoulder health, core. Rotator cuff priority.
Easy kick-only sets with fins. Develop feel.
200s and 400s at moderate pace. High volume, low rest.
Practise full rescue sequences at race effort - not just components.
8–10 × 50m fins at moderate effort. Build fin speed.
200m manikin carry reps. 400m rescue medley practice sets.
sessions.note: Core pool rescue events need repetition.

sessions.desc: 8–10 × 50m at faster than race pace. Full rest.
6 × 200m at threshold effort. 30 sec rest.
Full tube rescue sequence × 4–5 reps. Race speed.
sessions.note: Errors at this pace get ironed out now.

sessions.desc: 6 × 50m fins at sprint pace. Also 4 × 100m fins aerobic.
sessions.note: Fin events need both speed and endurance.

sessions.desc: 12 × 50m at race pace. Full rest between (60 sec+).
sessions.note: Once weekly. This is your race load.

sessions.desc: 6 × 100m + 4 × 200m across the week. Pace specific.
sessions.note: Pool rescue spans 50–200m - you need speed at all distances.

sessions.desc: Full heat sequence for each event. Treat it like a race.
8 × 50m fins at race pace. Full rest.
sessions.note: If you are pool 1 or pool 2 position.

sessions.desc: 6 × 50m race pace. Not 12. Stay fresh and sharp.
Full rescue sequence once per event at race effort.
10–15 min on start block reaction, turns, touch mechanics.
Full rest day before comp. Light activation morning of race.
